prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_FT_rf', seasonality_champion='THETA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation folds show mixed trend directions (2 down, 1 up) with significant seasonal amplitude (~7000). Model disagreement is extremely high (rmse_spread_ratio=2.78) and seasonality capture varies dramatically across models (variance=0.92). Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. Given the extreme model disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination with mixed weighting is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across different model families.

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ONLY_FT_rf",
  "seasonality_champion": "THETA",
  "overall_champion": "NaiveMovingAverage",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "DWT_svr", "late": "svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["NaiveMovingAverage", "ONLY_FT_svr", "ARIMA", "THETA", "ETS", "ONLY_FT_rf", "rf", "DWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["svr", "CWT_catboost", "catboost", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "DWT_svr", "NaiveSeasonal", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 2.7815,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["down", "down", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show mixed trend directions (2 down, 1 up) with significant seasonal amplitude (~7000). Model disagreement is extremely high (rmse_spread_ratio=2.78) and seasonality capture varies dramatically across models (variance=0.92). Early horizon specialists differ from late horizon specialists, indicating horizon-specific performance patterns. Given the extreme model disagreement and high seasonality variance, DBA combination with mixed weighting is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across different model families."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.35", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. relative_spread_mean=0.557 (≥0.25) and rmse_spread_ratio=2.78 signal extreme model disagreement, favoring dba_combination for phase-shift robustness. n_windows=3 is very small, so top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.35 prevents overfitting. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination explicitly for high seasonality variance (variance=0.92). Best single model by validation captures trend champions while per-horizon selection leverages early/late horizon specialists."
}
